subject: falzflyer-p2-mein-plan

hypotheses.name: Three items, each with one-sentence body
hypotheses.rationale: Drop two slogans, give the surviving three an explanatory sentence each. The list stops feeling like a checklist and starts feeling like an argument structure: claim, claim, claim — each with substance.
hypotheses.expected_outcome: Higher transport (clearer reasoning, less hand-wave); slightly lower visual punch but more credibility.

hypotheses.name: Single item privileged with Gelb backing
hypotheses.rationale: One slogan gets a full-bleed Gelb panel; the other four stay on Hellgrün at smaller scale beneath. The brand's accent color does the hierarchy work — the reader sees the privileged item before reading any text, and the rest functions as supporting evidence.
hypotheses.expected_outcome: High appeal from accent contrast; transport depends on whether viewers accept the prioritised item as the leading argument.

hypotheses.name: Cornerstone slogan in Vollkorn italic, rest in Gotham
hypotheses.rationale: Mixing typefaces creates priority through register rather than color or scale: the cornerstone item ('Klimaplan jetzt.') is set in Vollkorn italic at 28pt while the remaining four stay in Gotham 14pt. The reader's eye goes to the editorial voice first, then descends into the supporting list.
hypotheses.expected_outcome: Higher transport (clear entry point, single argument leads); appeal split by readers' affinity for editorial vs. activist register.

hypotheses.name: Left-aligned items separated by thin Dunkelgrün rules
hypotheses.rationale: Items hang on a left-axis grid, occupying the left two-thirds; thin horizontal rules separate them; the right third is intentional whitespace. The composition itself signals editorial seriousness rather than political-flyer flatness.
hypotheses.expected_outcome: Higher appeal among design-literate raters; transport benefits from the implicit 'this is considered, not shouted' frame.

hypotheses.name: Convert slogans to first-person commitments
hypotheses.rationale: 'Klimaplan jetzt.' becomes 'Ich verpflichte mich zum Klimaplan.' Each item turns from a slogan into a contract with a named candidate. The voice change reframes the panel from party platform to personal accountability.
hypotheses.expected_outcome: Higher transport via personal stake; appeal may suffer from reduced punchiness (longer phrases, more text to read).

hypotheses.name: Numbered 1–5 with weighted scale per rank
hypotheses.rationale: Items get explicit numerals (1–5) and decreasing type weight: #1 at 32pt Bold, #5 at 14pt Regular. The numerical and typographic ladder forces a reading sequence and tells the viewer which item the party considers most important.
hypotheses.expected_outcome: Higher transport (explicit ordering replaces ambiguity); appeal mixed — some readers will resist being told what is most important.

hypotheses.name: Replace the list with one editorial manifesto sentence
hypotheses.rationale: Five peer slogans dilute attention; collapsing the panel to a single 48pt Vollkorn Black statement ('Ein Plan für Niederösterreich') forces the panel to make one argument instead of five. The reduction is the design — scarcity creates weight where the list created flatness.
hypotheses.expected_outcome: Likely higher appeal from stronger identity, with transport split between readers who prefer a singular message and those who want concrete points.

hypotheses.name: Two items, panel mostly empty
hypotheses.rationale: Keep only two slogans, each set at 24pt with at least 60% of the panel as whitespace. Confidence-by-restraint: the panel claims authority through what it leaves out, signalling 'we don't need to list everything to be serious.'
hypotheses.expected_outcome: Higher appeal from the negative-space confidence; transport mixed — some will read it as bold, others as thin or evasive.

hypotheses.name: Privilege one item as hero statement
hypotheses.rationale: Promotes the first slogan to a dominant font size with a bright yellow (Gelb) background block, forcing it as the primary entry point while the remaining four items are grouped as secondary points below. This replaces the flat 'checklist' feel with a clear visual narrative.
hypotheses.expected_outcome: Higher appeal and significantly improved entry point; clearer understanding of the core priority.

hypotheses.name: Single attributed resident quote replaces slogans
hypotheses.rationale: The slogan list is replaced by one pull-quote from a named NÖ resident ('Maria, 54, Krems: "Mir fehlt eine Partei, die..."'). The argument is now told from outside the party rather than declared from within it.
hypotheses.expected_outcome: Higher transport via third-party credibility; appeal high if quote feels authentic, low if it reads as staged.

hypotheses.name: Staggered reverse-contrast blocks
hypotheses.rationale: Each slogan is placed within a Dunkelgrün box with white text. The boxes are of varying widths based on text length and are staggered horizontally across the grid, breaking the rigid verticality and injecting energy into the layout.
hypotheses.expected_outcome: High appeal and visual energy; the variation in alignment keeps the reader's eye moving through the list.

hypotheses.name: Handwritten marker text on torn-paper background
hypotheses.rationale: The five items are rendered in scanned handwritten marker on a torn-paper texture overlay, breaking every brand-typography rule. The panel deliberately abandons the polished party-flyer aesthetic to read as grassroots / protest / unfiltered urgency.
hypotheses.expected_outcome: Polarising — strong appeal from voters allergic to political-marketing polish, strong rejection from those expecting institutional credibility; transport spikes for the urgency frame.

notes: | Trimmed from 29 raw to 12 implemented variants for MVP execution (issue #29 T13).
